diagrams.description: This flowchart depicts the systematic approach of Game Theory. Beginning with the goal of predicting outcomes and determining optimal strategies, it proceeds to define a game through players, strategies, payoffs, and information. The game is classified into types (cooperative vs non-cooperative, zero-sum, simultaneous vs sequential, perfect/imperfect info). Solution concepts are then applied, including Nash Equilibrium and refinements like Subgame Perfect Nash, Bayesian Nash, and Evolutionary Stable Strategy. The resulting strategic insights inform applications across economics, politics, biology, and computer science.
